questions.q: Who broke the great bow at the swayamvara?
questions.explanation: Rama lifted and broke Shiva's bow at the swayamvara to win Sita.

questions.q: Whose hand in marriage was won by breaking the bow?
questions.explanation: Breaking the bow won Rama the hand of Sita.

questions.q: The bow that Rama broke originally belonged to which god?
questions.explanation: The bow was the famous bow of Shiva, kept in Janaka's court.

questions.q: Who was the father of Sita who hosted the swayamvara?
questions.explanation: King Janaka of Mithila was Sita's father and held the swayamvara.

questions.q: What was the condition Janaka set for marrying Sita?
questions.explanation: The suitor had to lift and string Shiva's mighty bow to win Sita.

questions.q: What happened to the bow when Rama drew it to string it?
questions.explanation: As Rama bent the bow to string it, it snapped in two with a thunderous sound.

questions.q: Which type of ceremony was the gathering where the bow was broken?
questions.explanation: The event was Sita's swayamvara, a ceremony to choose her husband.

questions.q: In which kingdom did the breaking of the bow take place?
questions.explanation: The swayamvara was held in Mithila, the kingdom of King Janaka.

questions.q: Which brother accompanied Rama to Mithila for the swayamvara?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana accompanied Rama on the journey with the sage to Mithila.

questions.q: What did breaking the bow announce about Rama?
questions.explanation: The feat revealed Rama's more-than-mortal strength to the assembled kings.

questions.q: Which sage brought Rama and Lakshmana to Janaka's court?
questions.explanation: The sage Vishvamitra led Rama and Lakshmana to Mithila where the bow was kept.

questions.q: What was the name of Shiva's bow kept in Janaka's court?
questions.explanation: Shiva's bow was named Pinaka, which no king could even lift.

questions.q: How was the heavy bow transported into the assembly for the test?
options: Drawn in on a large eight-wheeled chest
questions.explanation: The bow was brought in resting in a great eight-wheeled iron casket pulled by many men.

questions.q: Before Rama, how had countless kings fared at the bow?
options: They strung it but missed the target
They could not even lift or stir it
questions.explanation: Many kings had failed because they could not so much as lift or move the colossal bow.

questions.q: How had the divine bow come into the keeping of Janaka's family?
options: It was forged by Janaka's smiths
It was a gift from Shiva to Janaka's ancestors
It was won in war from Ravana
It fell from the sky during a storm
questions.explanation: The bow was given by Shiva and had been preserved as a sacred heirloom by Janaka's royal line.

questions.q: Who is the father of Rama, the prince who broke the bow?
questions.explanation: Rama was the son of King Dasharatha of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Sita is also known by which name connected to her father's kingdom or her origin?
questions.explanation: Sita is called Maithili because she is the princess of Mithila.

questions.q: What accompanied the snapping of the bow that startled the assembly?
questions.explanation: The bow broke with a tremendous crash like a thunderbolt, stunning those present.

questions.q: After the bow broke, what did Janaka resolve to do?
options: Give Sita to Rama in marriage
questions.explanation: Janaka joyfully resolved to give Sita to Rama, the only one to fulfill the vow.

questions.q: Messengers were sent after the feat to invite whom to Mithila?
questions.explanation: Janaka sent envoys to Ayodhya to invite Dasharatha and his family for the wedding.

questions.q: How is Sita traditionally said to have been found by Janaka?
options: Discovered in a furrow while plowing
questions.explanation: Sita was found by Janaka in a furrow of the earth while plowing a sacrificial field.

questions.q: What followed soon after Rama broke the bow at Mithila?
options: The marriage of Rama and Sita
questions.explanation: With the bow broken, the marriage of Rama and Sita was celebrated at Mithila.

questions.q: In Valmiki's account, how many men were said to draw the chest holding the bow?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Ramayana says the eight-wheeled casket bearing the bow was dragged by some five thousand strong men.

questions.q: Which sage in Janaka's narration had originally received Shiva's bow as a trust?
questions.explanation: Janaka relates that the bow was entrusted by the gods to his ancestor Devarata and preserved thereafter.

questions.q: Whose former bow, paired with Shiva's, is recalled in the lore surrounding this event?
questions.explanation: Tradition pairs Shiva's Pinaka with Vishnu's bow (Sharanga), a theme that resurfaces when Parashurama later appears.

questions.q: Shortly after the marriage, which fierce sage confronted Rama over the breaking of Shiva's bow?
questions.explanation: On the journey home, Parashurama angrily challenged Rama, testing him with Vishnu's bow after the breaking of Shiva's bow.

questions.q: What did Janaka vow about the suitor as a 'bride-price' or condition tied to the bow?
options: Only one of divine strength could win Sita
Sita would wed the richest king
Sita would choose by garland alone
questions.explanation: Janaka had vowed that Sita, born not of woman, would be given only to one who could wield the bow, proving more-than-human strength.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which other princesses were married alongside Sita after this event?
options: Sita's sister Urmila and cousins Mandavi and Shrutakirti
questions.explanation: Urmila wed Lakshmana, while Janaka's nieces Mandavi and Shrutakirti wed Bharata and Shatrughna in the joint ceremony.

questions.q: What did Rama do to the bow that no previous suitor had managed even to begin?
options: He lifted it, bent it, and strung it
He cast it into a fire
questions.explanation: Rama not only lifted the bow but bent it to string it, a stage no earlier king reached, and it broke in the bending.

questions.q: Within the larger Ramayana, the bow's breaking marks an early sign of which truth about Rama?
options: That he is an avatar of Vishnu
That he is a forest hermit
That he is the son of Janaka
That he is destined to rule Lanka
questions.explanation: The superhuman feat foreshadows Rama's identity as an incarnation of Vishnu, hinted throughout the epic.
